title: Detect and confirm distribution drift
description: Compare current feature distributions to a baseline with a statistical test, then confirm it is real before acting.
body: Steps: 1. Establish a reference baseline (e.g. the training window or a stable period). 2. Compute a drift metric per feature (population stability index, KS test, or mean/variance shift). 3. Flag features exceeding a tolerance. 4. Confirm the shift is genuine by ruling out a pipeline bug and checking sample size. 5. Decide the response: investigate source, adjust thresholds, or trigger retraining. Rollback point: not applicable — this is monitoring. Validation: flagged drift is reproduced against the baseline and survives a pipeline-integrity check; treating noise or a small sample as drift causes needless churn.
